audit_items.proof_trace: In nfp_cpp_area_cache_add, if kzalloc for cache fails (L807), the previously allocated area is not immediately freed — however, nfp_cpp_area_alloc_with_name registers the area into cpp->resource_list, and nfp_cpp_free explicitly iterates and frees all dangling areas from resource_list (L179-196), so there is no permanent memory leak. All other allocation sites (nfp_cpp_from_operations, nfp_cpp_area_alloc_with_name, nfp_cpp_explicit_acquire, __nfp_cpp_read, __nfp_cpp_write) correctly free memory on every error path.
